format_discipline.notes: First non-empty line is the '# Similarity scoring — computing cosine similarity between sentence pairs' H1; no leading YAML frontmatter. Sidecar reading.meta.json present and parses with word_count (1180), estimated_read_minutes (6), citations_used ([1,2,3]), sections. Exactly the six required '##' headings, in order: Overview, Key Concepts, Worked Example, In Practice, Key Takeaways, References. No added/renamed/dropped sections.

length.notes: SOFT below_band, within tolerance — NOT a hard miss. Prose word count (excluding the ```mermaid``` block L37-61) is ~1,180 words per the sidecar, ~20 words (1.7%) under the STANDARD 1200-1600 word floor. Read-aloud at the skill's 230 wpm = ~5.1 min; at the config's 200 wpm = 5.9 min, which the meta rounds to the in-band 6 min. Course rule is intuition-only (no math derivation / NumPy) and the corpus was deliberately short, so the trim is honest non-padding, not a content gap. Padding to clear the floor by 20 words would degrade the reading. Treated as in-band with a minor below-floor note, consistent with the 8.1 sibling precedent where read-aloud sat under the floor and the word band was allowed to govern. Flagged for orchestrator visibility: the ONLY soft miss is this ~20-word under-floor count.

citations.notes: Primary resource [1] (milvus.io) cited repeatedly (L7, L15, L31, L33, L35, L66, L75). Supporting [2] (L18, L79, L101, L104) and [3] (L66, L101, L103, L105) both cited inline. All three inline markers resolve to References entries 1-3; no orphan markers, no orphan bibliography entries, no fabricated markers. Citation density reasonable for a beginner reading.

forward_references.notes: No forward references. precision/recall (8.3+) absent. Euclidean distance not introduced. No math derivation, no NumPy, no by-hand computation. The single dot-product mention (L35) is intuition-only — 'one way people describe how the score is worked out is the dot product ... divided by their lengths — but you never compute that by hand. The lab tool does it' — matching corpus L62 and the resources note to skip heavy math. Embedding reused from prerequisite 6.7 without re-teaching, which is allowed.

faithfulness.notes: Sampled 12 assertions (text-a-friend opener, sentence embedding = one number-list, similar meaning -> similar number-lists, pre-computed vectors handed ready-made, arrow/angle picture, direction-not-length, score table 1/0.5/0/below-0, 0-1 typical range with full -1..1 scale, password/login high-score example, movie/pizza lower-score example, 6-step scoring flow, search/ticket-grouping/paraphrase use cases, do/don't habits). Every one traces verbatim or as a faithful paraphrase to topic_corpus.md. Zero unsupported assertions — the reading is a tight re-section of the corpus's 11 numbered sections into the six canonical headings.

beginner_readability.notes: Matches the fresher-zero-background baseline. Each non-everyday term (sentence embedding, pre-computed vectors, cosine similarity) defined in plain language at first use with bold-term/plain-meaning shape; concrete-first (text-a-friend opener, cat/feline sentences, arrows) before abstraction; short sentences, active voice, 'you' address. No cold acronyms. No paragraph stacks 3+ new terms. No hedging phrases.

formatting.notes: Scannable: bold-term definitions, bulleted concept lists, two comparison tables (score scale; same-words-vs-same-meaning), a numbered 6-step procedure in Worked Example, do/don't bullets in In Practice, inline examples beside concepts. No paragraph exceeds ~4 sentences; no wall_of_text; no unlisted_sequence. Inline examples appear outside the Worked Example section.

scope.notes: On the headline ('computing cosine similarity between sentence pairs'). No scope_creep: stays at intuition depth, defers model internals ('How a model turns text into those vectors was covered earlier and is not repeated here'), no precision/recall, no Euclidean, no math derivation. No LMS/assessment references (no assessment IDs, due weeks, or weights). Nothing taught beyond the topic title at STANDARD depth.

diagram_embed.notes: A single inline ```mermaid``` flowchart (L37-61) sits at the 'cosine similarity — the angle between two arrows' heart-of-topic point — a sensible location — contrasting small-angle/high-score vs wide-angle/low-score paths into a 0-1 scale. Followed immediately by an italic caption (L62). Consistent with meta diagram_embedded=true / embeds_diagram=true.

verdict: PASS. Reading 8.2 meets all reading-critic checks. Format clean (H1 first, no frontmatter, six canonical sections in order, sidecar valid). The ONLY soft miss is length: prose is ~1,180w, roughly 20 words (1.7%) under the STANDARD 1200-1600 word floor, while read-aloud minutes round to the in-band 6 min (config 200 wpm). Because the course rule is intuition-only (no math derivation / NumPy) and the source corpus was deliberately short, this is honest non-padding, not a content gap — padding to clear the floor by ~20 words would hurt the reading. Treated as soft below_band within tolerance, mirroring the 8.1 sibling precedent. Primary [1] cited repeatedly; [1][2][3] all resolve with no orphans or fabricated markers. No forward references — precision/recall (8.3+), Euclidean depth, and any by-hand math/NumPy are all avoided; the lone dot-product mention is explicitly intuition-only. Faithful to corpus across 12 sampled assertions (zero unsupported). Beginner tone and formatting strong (concrete-first analogies, bold-term definitions, numbered procedure, two comparison tables, do/don't bullets). On-scope, no scope_creep, no assessment prose. Mermaid diagram embedded inline at the angle-arrows heart-of-topic with caption. Gold reading examples exist in .claude/references/gold-examples/reading/ (2-1, 3-4); this reading meets that bar for sectioning, beginner concrete-first tone, and formatting density. Orchestrator note: if the ~20-word under-floor must be cleared exactly, a targeted micro-regen of one paragraph is cheaper than a full rebuild — but accept-as-is is the recommended call given in-band minutes and honest no-pad.
